channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Pothole in opposite , 13 FARADAY AVENUE ( marked in blue square before however not completed., 14th April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to report a serious issue caused by a pothole on our road. The road is very busy, and whenever vehicles pass over the pothole, it creates strong vibrations that can be felt inside our bedroom and in the living room . We have recently moved into the property, but our neighbours have informed us that this issue has been reported multiple times in the past and has still not been resolved. We are concerned that the ongoing vibration could potentially cause damage to our property if not addressed promptly. We also understand that previous reports mentioned difficulty in locating the pothole. To avoid any confusion, we kindly ask that when your team attends, they knock at our door (13/15 Faraday Avenue). We will be happy to show them the exact location of the problem. We would appreciate it if this matter could be treated with urgency and the pothole repaired as soon as possible. Thank you for your attention and consideration.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Faraday Avenue, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can St Blaise be inspected for potholes and road surface patching needed?, 14th April
item.description:
Can this busy section of road used for parking for the town centre be inspected and made good if possible?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harwood Avenue, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged walkway, 14th April
item.description:
A metal section of the pathway boundary has come off. This could become a trip hazard which will impact people especially those in the vulnerable groups.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Douglas Way, New Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marmont Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Outside number 16 Alma street, 14th April
item.description:
I have twice tripped over this hole in the pavement - and the green paint spilt across four of the beautiful paving stones really needs to be cleaned off at the same time please
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Alma Street, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of old furniture/timber dumped in parking area, 13th April
item.description:
Lots of old furniture/timber dumped in parking area. Creating potential fire hazard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stubbs Drive, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shattered glass on the road, 13th April
item.description:
There was a vehicle accident on the street last week resulting in shattered glass that has been left on the road / carriageway. Nobody has come to clean up the glass, which is very dangerous for vehicle tyres and pedestrians. It is also located right next to a nursery and primary school. The shattered glass is located outside 27 Pembridge Villas, London, W113EP.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 27, Pembridge Villas, Notting Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked footpath, 13th April
item.description:
Parked for three weeks blocking pedestrians from using footpath, forcing them to walk in the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Catharine Close, Grays
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cycle path, 13th April
item.description:
Cycle paths are full of potholes on Carlton vale making it dangerous to ride esp with deep potholes all over on this street.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carlton Vale, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter in alleyway between Pembury Close and Farleigh Avenue, 13th April
item.description:
There is a large amount of litter in the alleyway that runs beween Pembury Close and Farleigh Avenue. Please would you arrange for all the litter to be picked up and removed as soon as possible.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pembury Close, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter and broken glass in Mounthurst Road, 13th April
item.description:
There is broken glass on the pavement outside 75 Mounthurst Road. Please would you arrange for this to be swept up and removed ASAP. There is a huge amount of litter on the pavement outside 57 Mounthurst Road. Please would you arrange for the litter to be picked up and removed ASAP.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mounthurst Road, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken, uneven paving stones outside 11/13 Farleigh Avenue, 13th April
item.description:
There are broken, uneven paving stones outside 11/13 Farleigh Avenue. Please would you carry out an inspection and arrange for the stones to be removed and replaced with new ones.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chilham Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter in Boughton Avenue, 13th April
item.description:
There is litter on the pavement opposite 27-29 Boughton Avenue. Please would you arrange for this litter to be picked up and removed as soon as possible.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Boughton Avenue, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on green BT box in Farleigh Avenue, 13th April
item.description:
There is a large amount of graffiti on the unnumbered green BT box in Farleigh Avenue (towards the junction with Eastry Avenue). This graffiti needs to be removed ASAP, otherwise it will multiply.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Farleigh Avenue, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cracked/sunken slabs, 13th April
item.description:
A few cracked slabs and one quite sunken. Not very obvious at night if the sign is blocking the view.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warwick Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter in tree pit outside 149 Bourne Vale, 13th April
item.description:
There is litter (including a dog waste bag) in the tree pit outside 149 Bourne Vale. Please would you see that this litter is picked up and removed ASAP.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Vale, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
